channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Shida Bazyar’s Novel Explores Iranian Revolution and Exile Across Generations
item.description: Shida Bazyar’s novel 'The Nights are Quiet in Tehran', translated from German by Ruth Martin, has been shortlisted for the 2026 International Booker Prize. The story spans three decades from 1979 to 2...

item.title: The curious case of Anders Fernstedt, the homeless man living outside London's largest house
item.description: The story of Anders Fernstedt, a homeless Swedish man, who has become the only resident of London’s largest private home, has captured the imagination of locals and visitors alike. Located at 2-8a Rut...

item.title: KZN Premier condemns brutal killing of seven Moonsamy family members and calls for community action
item.description: KwaZulu-Natal Premier Thamsanqa Ntuli has strongly condemned the brutal killing of seven members of the Moonsamy family, describing the tragedy as a devastating blow to the province. Speaking at a mas...

item.title: Funke Awodiya’s Celebration of Womanhood in Poetry
item.description: This article na review of Funke Awodiya’s poetry book titled 'Woman of a Woman', as Syl Cheney Coker take look am. The book get sixty pages full of poems wey dey celebrate woman for all her aspects. T...

item.title: Australian artist Peter Godwin wins $100k prize for Li River landscape despite initial doubts
item.description: Australian artist Peter Godwin has taken out a $100,000 art prize for a work inspired by the iconic karst mountain landscapes along China’s Li River, a subject long revered in traditional Chinese art....

item.title: Trump's Plan to Bail Out Spirit Airlines Sparks Division Within His Party
item.description: President Donald Trump's suggestion this week to 'just buy' bankrupt Spirit Airlines has led to significant debate within the Republican Party. While Trump argued that a bailout would protect jobs and...

item.title: No Breakthrough in US–Iran Peace Talks, Tensions Continue
item.description: The peace talks between the US and Iran show no signs of progress, as President Donald Trump cancelled a visit by US envoys to Pakistan, which had been intended to mediate the conflict. The decision c...

item.title: Police say blast outside station was a deliberate attempt to target officers
item.description: The article reports on an incident in which an explosive device detonated outside a police station, with authorities indicating that the act was a deliberate attempt to kill police officers. According...

item.title: Sabastian Sawe breaks two-hour marathon barrier to win London Marathon in record time
item.description: Kenya’s Sabastian Sawe has made athletics history by becoming the first athlete to officially run a marathon in under two hours, clocking 1:59:30 at the 2026 London Marathon. The 29-year-old not only ...

item.title: A Collection of Political Cartoons for 27 April 2026
item.description: This gallery presents a curated selection of political cartoons published on 27 April 2026, reflecting current events and political commentary in Australia. Readers can browse through three featured i...

item.title: The Importance of Effort and Presence in Maintaining Friendships
item.description: The article explores the tension between modern convenience culture and the effort required to maintain meaningful friendships. Through personal anecdotes and expert insights, it highlights how acts o...

item.title: Details of the Shooting Incident at the White House Correspondents' Dinner
item.description: A shooting occurred during the White House Correspondents' Dinner, a high-profile event attended by journalists, politicians, and celebrities. The incident disrupted the evening, with initial reports ...

item.title: Limpopo High Court rejects woman's appeal over pension forfeiture claim in divorce
item.description: A Limpopo woman’s appeal to overturn a divorce ruling denying her claim for forfeiture of matrimonial benefits was dismissed by the High Court in Polokwane. The case stemmed from a 35-year marriage wh...

item.title: Trump comments on shooting incident at White House media dinner
item.description: US President Donald Trump spoke publicly after a shooter caused chaos at the White House Correspondents' Dinner in Washington on Saturday. Responding to a reporter's question about why such incidents ...

item.title: The Influence of Culture, Marketing, and Western Templates on Book Cover Design in India
item.description: In India, book cover design is a complex interplay of regional visual cultures, Western design templates, and market demands. Indian designers are faced with the challenge of balancing indigenous aest...

item.title: Furniture brand showcases the use of charred wood for premium local design
item.description: A furniture brand has taken an innovative approach in incorporating charred wood into its designs, giving local luxury furniture a more sophisticated and unique touch. By utilizing a traditional metho...

item.title: Seth Fedelin affirms support for fellow love teams amid upcoming film projects
item.description: In a recent statement, actor Seth Fedelin clarified that there is no rivalry between his team-up and other popular love teams such as JMFyang and DonBelle, especially as multiple film projects are bei...

item.title: Details emerge about suspect and charges after shooting at White House Correspondents' Dinner
item.description: Cole Tomas Allen, a 31-year-old teacher from Torrance, California, has been identified as the suspect in a shooting incident at the White House Correspondents' Dinner in Washington, DC. Authorities st...

item.title: Delhi police open fire on labourers, resulting in one death and one injury
item.description: According to reports, a tragic incident occurred in Delhi where a police officer reportedly fired at a group of labourers, leading to the death of one individual and injuring another. The circumstance...

item.title: PM Modi Criticises West Bengal Government Ahead of 2026 Elections
item.description: The news article appears to cover a political speech delivered by Prime Minister Narendra Modi in West Bengal in the context of the upcoming 2026 state elections. Based on the title and available info...
